operating_state: Total revenues increased to $2.8 billion in Q2 2023 from $2.7 billion in Q2 2022, driven by property revenue growth to $2.7 billion with colocation expansion across most segments. U.S. & Canada property revenue grew from 119 million colocations and amendments, while Africa and Europe delivered double-digit growth. Asia-Pacific revenue declined due to Vodafone Idea Limited payment shortfalls in India creating revenue reserves. Data Centers revenue expanded from new lease commencements and customer expansions.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $1.9 billion with property costs rising driven by higher fuel costs in Africa and pass-through ground rent in Latin America. Depreciation and amortization decreased to $765 million from $827 million due to lower intangible asset amortization. Stock-based compensation expense increased following accelerated vesting schedule changes from four-year to three-year terms beginning March 2023. Other operating expenses surged primarily from losses on Mexico Fiber and Poland asset sales.
material_events: AMT completed Mexico Fiber sale generating $80 million loss and disposed of Poland subsidiary. The company issued $4.2 billion in senior notes and completed $1.3 billion securitization transaction through American Tower Trust I backed by 5,036 tower sites. AMT repaid $1.7 billion in maturing debt and received $193 million face value convertible debentures from Vodafone Idea Limited as partial receivables settlement.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted revenue by $111 million from Indian Rupee and African currencies, partially offset by positive impact from Latin American currencies. The company faces elevated collection uncertainty in India with VIL making only partial payments despite Supreme Court AGR fee rulings affecting carrier financial health. Interest expense increased significantly to $348 million from $277 million driven by higher debt levels and rising rates.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2023 capital expenditures of $1.65-1.76 billion including construction of communications sites globally and data center assets. The company anticipates nearly $62 billion of non-cancellable customer lease revenue over future periods based on existing leases. AMT maintains $2.0 billion in authorized stock repurchase capacity and extended credit facility maturities to 2026-2028.
uncertainty_profile: India operations face continued uncertainty with VIL partial payments leading to $70 million revenue reserves and potential for additional asset impairments. The company is exploring strategic alternatives to reduce India exposure including potential equity interest sales subject to regulatory approvals. Securitization structures require maintenance of debt service coverage ratios above 1.30x while bank facilities mandate total leverage below 6.0x and senior secured leverage below 3.0x Adjusted EBITDA.
